channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse for India and global stories.

item.title: Saudi Aramco Q1 profit jumps 26% as key pipeline reaches capacity amid Iran war
item.description: Aramco says its East-West pipeline has helped to mitigate the energy shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Mythos | Double-edged sword
item.description: The Anthropic AI model can not only identify vulnerabilities in cyberspace that humans may have missed, but also generate ways to exploit them, potentially even for non-experts

item.title: US Awaits Iran Reply as Aramco Says Hormuz Opening No Quick Fix
item.description: The wait stretched on for Iran’s response to a US proposal to end 10 weeks of war, as the world’s largest oil company warned it would take months for the market to return to normal even if the Strait of Hormuz reopened immediately.

item.title: Maternal Health Beyond Childbirth: Doctor Explains Long-Term Care And Awareness Gaps
item.description: Long-term care for mothers after childbirth extends far beyond the immediate postpartum recovery period. It plays an important role in safeguarding physical, mental, and emotional well-being.

item.title: Sensex to hit 3 lakh by 2036? Raamdeo Agrawal says India is the 'Ferrari' among markets, here's why
item.description: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Chairman Raamdeo Agrawal said India remains one of the world’s best markets for long-term wealth creation despite global uncertainties and AI-led shifts. Speaking at the Groww India Investor Festival 2026, he highlighted India’s strong compounding story, rising financialisation and pote

item.title: Indian stock market: How are Sensex and Nifty 50 likely to perform next week amid US-Iran war uncertainty? - Mint
item.description: Indian stock market: How are Sensex and Nifty 50 likely to perform next week amid US-Iran war uncertainty? Mint

item.title: Chinese Consulate General in Kolkata issues renewed warning against unintentional Nepal-India border crossings - Global Times
item.description: Chinese Consulate General in Kolkata issues renewed warning against unintentional Nepal-India border crossings Global Times

item.title: HC protects MP Shashi Tharoor's personality rights, directs removal of deepfake video
item.description: The Delhi High Court has protected Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's personality rights. The court ordered X to remove an AI-generated deepfake video showing him praising Pakistan. It also restrained the misuse of his name, image, and voice for any purpose. Meta was asked to keep certain Instagram reels inaccessible. This o

item.title: 10 smallcap stocks soaring up to 116% in FY27. Do you own them?
item.description: Markets have shown recovery since April, with small-cap stocks significantly outperforming benchmarks. Several smallcap gainers have recorded substantial rallies, driven by factors like strong demand, strategic appointments, and positive regulatory developments.

item.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Coforge and NBCC on Monday for near-term gains of up to 7%
item.description: The Nifty ended sharply lower amid escalating US-Iran tensions, rupee weakness and heavy selling in financial stocks, slipping below the 50-day EMA and signalling renewed weakness. Analysts expect further downside if the index remains below 24,200, while Coforge and NBCC (India) are seen as attractive short-term tradin

item.title: Traders looking for next leg in global stocks rally bet on Asia
item.description: Investors are increasingly betting on Asia for the next phase of the global equities rally, driven by enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and strong performances in South Korean and Taiwanese markets. Strategists remain bullish on AI-linked semiconductor and hardware stocks, while India lags due to oil dependence

item.title: Zepto, Dhoot Transmission among 6 companies to secure Sebi's nod for IPO
item.description: As many as six companies, including quick commerce unicorn Zepto and auto components manufacturer Dhoot Transmission, have secured Sebi's approval to raise funds through initial public offerings (IPOs).

item.title: Anwar to Outline Malaysia Oil Supply Continuity Plan: Bernama
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will unveil a plan soon to shore up the country’s oil supply as uncertainties stemming from the conflict in Iran continue, Bernama reported.

item.title: Anwar to Outline Malaysia Oil Supply Continuity Plan: Bernama
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will unveil a plan soon to shore up the country’s oil supply as uncertainties stemming from the conflict in Iran continue, Bernama reported.

item.title: ‘Missiles, drones locked on US targets’: Iran warns of ‘heavy assault’ after tanker strikes in Gulf of Oman
item.description: Iran's IRGC has warned of a "heavy assault" on US military assets and ships if Iranian oil tankers or commercial vessels are attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. This follows US forces disabling two Iranian oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman and an earlier exchange of fire near the strait.

item.title: Chalmers on 'Unacceptable' Australian Housing Status Quo
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the 2026-27 Federal Budget on May 12. It's framed as one the country's most consequential Budgets against a backdrop of inflation-fuelling global energy shocks, climbing cost-of-living, slowing productivity and a national debt edging toward A$1 trillion. Chalmers speaks

item.title: Chalmers on 'Unacceptable' Australian Housing Status Quo
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the 2026-27 Federal Budget on May 12. It's framed as one the country's most consequential Budgets against a backdrop of inflation-fuelling global energy shocks, climbing cost-of-living, slowing productivity and a national debt edging toward A$1 trillion. Chalmers speaks with Bloomberg's James Mayger in Canberra. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Cybersecurity entering AI-vs-AI era as attackers, defenders deploy autonomous systems: WEF report
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is shifting to an "AI versus AI" era as attackers leverage artificial intelligence for faster, more sophisticated threats. In response, organizations are deploying AI-driven systems for autonomous threat detection and response, significantly improving efficiency and reducing breach costs.

item.title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
item.description: The attack on the Trellix source code repository disclosed last week has been claimed by the RansomHouse threat group, which leaked a small set of images as proof of the intrusion. [...]

item.title: Aramco CEO Warns of Long Oil Market Disruption as Profit Jumps
item.description: Saudi Aramco’s boss warned of a long disruption to oil markets from the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz, while the company reported a jump in profit following higher prices and its ability to redirect exports via a pipeline bypassing the vital waterway.

item.title: Two wins, two losses: What India, Pakistan have learned a year after war
item.description: India and Pakistan each claim strategic successes after the four-day conflict, as tensions continue to simmer.

item.title: T.N. Government formation: IUML endorses M.K. Stalin’s demand for a new government
item.description: Professor Kader Mohideen, the leader of the party, said his party has not given a letter of support to Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam, led by actor Vijay.

item.title: Stalin reacts to Tamil Nadu CM Vijay speech: ‘Money is there; what is required is efficient administration’
item.description: Responding to Mr. Vijay’s claim that his electoral promises were practical, Mr. Stalin said the new Chief Minister had only just entered administration and would now learn the challenges involved in fulfilling promises.

item.title: Video | Russia Ukraine War | Ukraine War “Heading To An End”, Says Putin Amid Peace Push - NDTV
item.description: Video | Russia Ukraine War | Ukraine War “Heading To An End”, Says Putin Amid Peace Push NDTV

item.title: Aramco Profit Tops Estimates After War-Driven Oil Price Rise
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported first-quarter profit that surpassed analysts’ expectations following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels.

item.title: Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 26% as Iran War Drives Oil Prices
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported a 26% jump in first-quarter profit following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels, and as it redirected exports via a pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pakistan central bank hikes policy rate by 100 bps as West Asia war fuels inflation - MSN
item.description: Pakistan central bank hikes policy rate by 100 bps as West Asia war fuels inflation MSN

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. In 2026, hackers are using AI to make these "first clicks" nearly impossible to spot. If a single laptop get

item.title: India to build strategic outpost near key shipping lane in Indo-Pacific
item.description: “This is what I call the Hormuz Strait effect," one analyst said, referring to the collapse of shipping through the vital waterway due to war.

item.title: 'Gave wrong addresses': Delhi Police on accused students in India Gate protest case; calls move 'deliberate' - MSN
item.description: 'Gave wrong addresses': Delhi Police on accused students in India Gate protest case; calls move 'deliberate' MSN
